description_long: This object is a cylindrical stone block with a surface texture resembling white-painted brickwork, giving it a robust and urban appearance. The block is topped with a flat, dark, almost black circular cap that has a subtle greenish edge, suggesting a possible metal or treated stone finish. The brick pattern is consistent and evenly distributed around the curved sides, with mortar lines clearly visible, adding to the realism of the masonry. The top surface is smooth and untextured, contrasting with the rougher, tactile sides. There are no visible openings, engravings, or functional parts, indicating it is a solid barrier. Its size and finish suggest it could serve as a decorative or functional obstacle in public urban spaces such as plazas, sidewalks, or parks.
description: A cylindrical stone block with a white brick-patterned exterior and a smooth, dark circular top, designed as a robust urban barrier.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cylindrical shape, white brick texture, and the dark, flat top with a slight greenish edge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the continuous brick pattern and the seamless transition to the dark top surface.
description_view_2: Rear view with consistent brickwork and a clear view of the smooth, dark top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the uniform brick pattern and the flat, dark top surface.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a protective or decorative barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or mortared in place
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the paint appears fresh and the brickwork is intact, with no chips or erosion.
